HI Marc,
 
Please find below the answers to your questions.
 
We have clients that are using AWS native tools for cost reporting and we are keen to understand when/what changes with their experience.
 
From your customer’s perspective, there should be no change to their experience when it comes to using AWS native cost reporting tools. Services such as Cost Explorer, CUR (Cost and Usage Reports), and other AWS-native billing and reporting capabilities will continue to function exactly as they do today.The transition to TD SYNNEX as the distribution partner primarily affects the billing and distribution relationship on the backend, not the operational environment within AWS.  
 
We are wondering if there is a two hop route to implementing this change and what the impact would be to our margin. Phase  1) just make TD our distribution , Phase 2) move some customers over to new model that removes Root for Distribution. 
 
Now, there isn’t a simple or automatic way to transfer accounts between different distribution models. While initiating the transition to TD SYNNEX as the distributor is certainly possible, moving accounts across structural models typically requires careful coordination and review on a case-by-case basis to ensure the transition is handled smoothly.
I would also recommend reviewing the exit clause with your current distributor. In most cases, partners are required to provide a 90-day notice period before making a move to another distributor. Ensuring this is aligned early in the process helps avoid any delays or contractual complications during the transition.
 
Kindly revert on any clarifications.
 

Thanks & Regards

Hi Christine, Gina & Rupesh
Please treat as priority and address comments in blue:
 
Custom cost-management tools and Security dashboards via StreamOne Ion?
Yes, the StreamOne platform provides pre-built dashboards and the ability to create custom reports, including configurable alerts. These reports can show usage, consumption trends, and other financial insights.
StreamOne also offers real-time reporting on IaaS consumption (Azure, AWS, and Google), enabling advanced cost-optimization capabilities and continuous billing visibility, not just month-end reporting.
In addition, StreamOne includes a SecOps Portal for partners, providing a centralized dashboard to view and manage customer security features and policies.
If you need more specific details, I’m happy to follow up.
Please provide detailed steps how you will assist us with the change-over from another aggregator to TDS? 
Access to AWS funding programs, ACE deal registrations and leads?
Yes, As a Premier Tier AWS Partner, we help our partners leverage AWS mechanisms and programs, including offerings, funding, enablement, and guidance on ACE deal registrations as part of co-sell execution (where eligible and aligned to AWS criteria).
The TD SYNNEX AWS team also has a dedicated Customer Success and Conversion team focused on customer lead generation and demand generation. Who exactly would be part of our team? Where does Gina fit into the picture and how can we utilize her as a resource?
We’ve built an infrastructure framework around programs like SBAI and other third-party lead-gen mechanisms to maintain, hydrate, nurture, and route end-customer leads to our partners—across both Commercial and Public Sector.
API access or automated insights for billing data independent of AWS (i.e., aligning payment terms, managing outstanding balances are important to us)
Yes, both are available. I can connect you with the team that managed this process so they can provide a detailed overview and answer any specific questions.
What GTM strategies do you offer? Free training and discounted workshops? Co-sponsored AWS events?
Yes, TD SYNNEX offers several GTM motions to help partners build and scale pipeline, including enablement, co-marketing, and co-sell support.
Pipeline: Top-of-funnel, assessment-led sales plays for customers both on AWS and not yet on AWS.
Training: Fully subsidized training and enablement programs are available.
Events: Depending on the use case, eligibility, and cost, we can either fully subsidize or co-invest in joint webinars, SKOs, and customer-facing events—provided there’s a defined solution focus, target audience, pipeline target, and measurable ROI.
On average, how much MDF credits can we anticipate receiving monthly/annually from the TDSynnex program? We would like to see details as this is important to us.
Will we be assigned a dedicated account manager, technical expert or help with pre-sales and design? (saving strategies and rightsizing recommendations are critical).
Yes, you’ll have a dedicated AWS account manager, plus access to technical SMEs and pre-sales engineering resources. Again, who would be part of our team, Gina?
Given the breadth of AWS service delivery designations and competencies (e.g., MSP, migrations, government, education, to name a few), our AWS practice provides technical expertise and support across migrations & modernization, managed services, professional services, assessments, and AWS workloads.
Additionally, our ServiceSolv team provides engineering, integration, deployment, and remediation services which enables you to offer end-to-end customer lifecycle support without building these functions internally. We can also provide white-labeled resources delivered under your branding, where applicable.
Net 90 terms
Extended payment terms are feasible through TD SYNNEX. We'd have to get you in contact with our credit/finance team to review your eligibility If 90 is denied for whatever reason, what is your standard, 60? 2% Net 10?
If Aptum has an AWS customer that goes bankrupt or denies us payment, what programs do you have to reduce this burden? Historically, we’ve had to pay for AWS consumption and cannot collect from a bankrupt customer… 
Besides 10th of the following month billing; what other sweeteners can you add to incentivize Aptum to award TDSynnex over a competitor?

April 1st AWS Program Changes

Distributors move to back-end rebate only (no front-end discount)
New billing transfer program creates visibility silos between customer/partner/distributor
TDS Model:
100% pass-through: "You get what you earn"
No tiered programs—partners receive full earned discounts
Root exception & billing transfer capabilities ready now
Additional Opportunity: Fintech Customer Migration

Opportunity: TDS can build exception case based on historical migration work and APFP submissions
Billing transfer allows account capture while customer retains root access

Partner Growth Incentive lost for one year when switching distributors

Hello Aptum team,

Nice to e-meet you all.

Thank you for your due diligence, you’re asking all the right questions. I did my best to respond succinctly in email, but I’d love to walk through each point in more depth on a call, especially if you’re interested in how we differentiate compared to other AWS distributors.

Custom cost-management tools and Security dashboards via StreamOne Ion?

Yes, the StreamOne platform provides pre-built dashboards and the ability to create custom reports, including configurable alerts. These reports can show usage, consumption trends, and other financial insights.

StreamOne also offers real-time reporting on IaaS consumption (Azure, AWS, and Google), enabling advanced cost-optimization capabilities and continuous billing visibility, not just month-end reporting.

In addition, StreamOne includes a SecOps Portal for partners, providing a centralized dashboard to view and manage customer security features and policies.

If you need more specific details, I’m happy to follow up.

Access to AWS funding programs, ACE deal registrations and leads?

Yes, As a Premier Tier AWS Partner, we help our partners leverage AWS mechanisms and programs, including offerings, funding, enablement, and guidance on ACE deal registrations as part of co-sell execution (where eligible and aligned to AWS criteria).

The TD SYNNEX AWS team also has a dedicated Customer Success and Conversion team focused on customer lead generation and demand generation.

We’ve built an infrastructure framework around programs like SBAI and other third-party lead-gen mechanisms to maintain, hydrate, nurture, and route end-customer leads to our partners—across both Commercial and Public Sector.

API access or automated insights for billing data independent of AWS (i.e., aligning payment terms, managing outstanding balances are important to us)

Yes, both are available. I can connect you with the team that managed this process so they can provide a detailed overview and answer any specific questions.

What GTM strategies do you offer? Free training and discounted workshops? Co-sponsored AWS events?

Yes, TD SYNNEX offers several GTM motions to help partners build and scale pipeline, including enablement, co-marketing, and co-sell support.

Pipeline: Top-of-funnel, assessment-led sales plays for customers both on AWS and not yet on AWS.
Training: Fully subsidized training and enablement programs are available.
Events: Depending on the use case, eligibility, and cost, we can either fully subsidize or co-invest in joint webinars, SKOs, and customer-facing events—provided there’s a defined solution focus, target audience, pipeline target, and measurable ROI.

Will we be assigned a dedicated account manager, technical expert or help with pre-sales and design? (saving strategies and rightsizing recommendations are critical).

Yes, you’ll have a dedicated AWS account manager, plus access to technical SMEs and pre-sales engineering resources.

Given the breadth of AWS service delivery designations and competencies (e.g., MSP, migrations, government, education, to name a few), our AWS practice provides technical expertise and support across migrations & modernization, managed services, professional services, assessments, and AWS workloads.

Additionally, our ServiceSolv team provides engineering, integration, deployment, and remediation services which enables you to offer end-to-end customer lifecycle support without building these functions internally. We can also provide white-labeled resources delivered under your branding, where applicable.

Net 90 terms

Extended payment terms are feasible through TD SYNNEX. We'd have to get you in contact with our credit/finance team to review your eligibility 

 

Thanks,

Michael

We have clients that are using AWS native tools for cost reporting and we are keen to understand when/what changes with their experience.
 
From your customer’s perspective, there should be no change to their experience when it comes to using AWS native cost reporting tools. Services such as Cost Explorer, CUR (Cost and Usage Reports), and other AWS-native billing and reporting capabilities will continue to function exactly as they do today.The transition to TD SYNNEX as the distribution partner primarily affects the billing and distribution relationship on the backend, not the operational environment within AWS.  
 
We are wondering if there is a two hop route to implementing this change and what the impact would be to our margin. Phase  1) just make TD our distribution , Phase 2) move some customers over to new model that removes Root for Distribution. 
 
Now, there isn’t a simple or automatic way to transfer accounts between different distribution models. While initiating the transition to TD SYNNEX as the distributor is certainly possible, moving accounts across structural models typically requires careful coordination and review on a case-by-case basis to ensure the transition is handled smoothly.
I would also recommend reviewing the exit clause with your current distributor. In most cases, partners are required to provide a 90-day notice period before making a move to another distributor. Ensuring this is aligned early in the process helps avoid any delays or contractual complications during the transition.

Hi Christine, Gina & Rupesh
Please treat as priority and address comments in blue:
 
Custom cost-management tools and Security dashboards via StreamOne Ion?
Yes, the StreamOne platform provides pre-built dashboards and the ability to create custom reports, including configurable alerts. These reports can show usage, consumption trends, and other financial insights.
StreamOne also offers real-time reporting on IaaS consumption (Azure, AWS, and Google), enabling advanced cost-optimization capabilities and continuous billing visibility, not just month-end reporting.
In addition, StreamOne includes a SecOps Portal for partners, providing a centralized dashboard to view and manage customer security features and policies.
If you need more specific details, I’m happy to follow up.
Please provide detailed steps how you will assist us with the change-over from another aggregator to TDS? 
Access to AWS funding programs, ACE deal registrations and leads?
Yes, As a Premier Tier AWS Partner, we help our partners leverage AWS mechanisms and programs, including offerings, funding, enablement, and guidance on ACE deal registrations as part of co-sell execution (where eligible and aligned to AWS criteria).
The TD SYNNEX AWS team also has a dedicated Customer Success and Conversion team focused on customer lead generation and demand generation. Who exactly would be part of our team? Where does Gina fit into the picture and how can we utilize her as a resource?
We’ve built an infrastructure framework around programs like SBAI and other third-party lead-gen mechanisms to maintain, hydrate, nurture, and route end-customer leads to our partners—across both Commercial and Public Sector.
API access or automated insights for billing data independent of AWS (i.e., aligning payment terms, managing outstanding balances are important to us)
Yes, both are available. I can connect you with the team that managed this process so they can provide a detailed overview and answer any specific questions.
What GTM strategies do you offer? Free training and discounted workshops? Co-sponsored AWS events?
Yes, TD SYNNEX offers several GTM motions to help partners build and scale pipeline, including enablement, co-marketing, and co-sell support.
Pipeline: Top-of-funnel, assessment-led sales plays for customers both on AWS and not yet on AWS.
Training: Fully subsidized training and enablement programs are available.
Events: Depending on the use case, eligibility, and cost, we can either fully subsidize or co-invest in joint webinars, SKOs, and customer-facing events—provided there’s a defined solution focus, target audience, pipeline target, and measurable ROI.
On average, how much MDF credits can we anticipate receiving monthly/annually from the TDSynnex program? We would like to see details as this is important to us.

Hello Aptum team,

Nice to e-meet you all.

Thank you for your due diligence, you’re asking all the right questions. I did my best to respond succinctly in email, but I’d love to walk through each point in more depth on a call, especially if you’re interested in how we differentiate compared to other AWS distributors.

Custom cost-management tools and Security dashboards via StreamOne Ion?

Yes, the StreamOne platform provides pre-built dashboards and the ability to create custom reports, including configurable alerts. These reports can show usage, consumption trends, and other financial insights.

StreamOne also offers real-time reporting on IaaS consumption (Azure, AWS, and Google), enabling advanced cost-optimization capabilities and continuous billing visibility, not just month-end reporting.

In addition, StreamOne includes a SecOps Portal for partners, providing a centralized dashboard to view and manage customer security features and policies.

If you need more specific details, I’m happy to follow up.

Access to AWS funding programs, ACE deal registrations and leads?

Yes, As a Premier Tier AWS Partner, we help our partners leverage AWS mechanisms and programs, including offerings, funding, enablement, and guidance on ACE deal registrations as part of co-sell execution (where eligible and aligned to AWS criteria).

The TD SYNNEX AWS team also has a dedicated Customer Success and Conversion team focused on customer lead generation and demand generation.

We’ve built an infrastructure framework around programs like SBAI and other third-party lead-gen mechanisms to maintain, hydrate, nurture, and route end-customer leads to our partners—across both Commercial and Public Sector.

API access or automated insights for billing data independent of AWS (i.e., aligning payment terms, managing outstanding balances are important to us)

Yes, both are available. I can connect you with the team that managed this process so they can provide a detailed overview and answer any specific questions.

What GTM strategies do you offer? Free training and discounted workshops? Co-sponsored AWS events?

Yes, TD SYNNEX offers several GTM motions to help partners build and scale pipeline, including enablement, co-marketing, and co-sell support.

Pipeline: Top-of-funnel, assessment-led sales plays for customers both on AWS and not yet on AWS.
Training: Fully subsidized training and enablement programs are available.
Events: Depending on the use case, eligibility, and cost, we can either fully subsidize or co-invest in joint webinars, SKOs, and customer-facing events—provided there’s a defined solution focus, target audience, pipeline target, and measurable ROI.

Will we be assigned a dedicated account manager, technical expert or help with pre-sales and design? (saving strategies and rightsizing recommendations are critical).

Yes, you’ll have a dedicated AWS account manager, plus access to technical SMEs and pre-sales engineering resources.

Given the breadth of AWS service delivery designations and competencies (e.g., MSP, migrations, government, education, to name a few), our AWS practice provides technical expertise and support across migrations & modernization, managed services, professional services, assessments, and AWS workloads.

Additionally, our ServiceSolv team provides engineering, integration, deployment, and remediation services which enables you to offer end-to-end customer lifecycle support without building these functions internally. We can also provide white-labeled resources delivered under your branding, where applicable.

Net 90 terms

Extended payment terms are feasible through TD SYNNEX. We'd have to get you in contact with our credit/finance team to review your eligibility

items.subject: April resources to drive renewals and operational readiness
items.body_text:
[Microsoft AI Cloud Partner Program]
Plus: Explore the latest incentives, skilling, and program updates.

Having trouble viewing this email? | View as a web page

 

 

 

Annual capabilities assessment: Refinements and interim approach
 
Microsoft is updating the annual Cloud Solution Provider (CSP) capabilities assessment experience for existing direct bill and distributor partners.
 

Review requirements 

 

After a partner meets the applicable eligibility requirements or remediates any at-risk items, they are considered compliant through the remainder of the cycle. The next eligibility requirements assessment will take place during the anniversary of the partner’s earliest onboarding month in the following year.
No action is required from partners for the assessment at this time, including partners whose earliest onboarded month occurred January through April 2026. We are finalizing an updated assessment and validation approach for a future release. If an updated assessment or validation step is introduced, partners will receive direct, official Microsoft communication in advance with clear instructions and timelines.
What partners should do now:
Keep your organization’s CSP enrollment information current in Partner Center, including Global Admin and Billing Admin contacts.
Continue to meet and maintain compliance with all applicable CSP eligibility requirements for your authorization. For additional detail and scenario examples, review the authorization FAQ.
Watch for direct, official Microsoft communication with instructions and timelines if an updated assessment or validation step is introduced.
New direct bill and distributor partners must still complete and pass the capabilities assessment when onboarding under a new authorization, expanding into an additional CSP market or region, or applying for a new authorization type.
Final reminder: Extended service terms enforcement begins May 4 
Effective May 4, 2026, we're aligning renewal and end-of-term options to create a more consistent and predictable experience. Customers and partners will have clear choices at expiration: renew to a new term, cancel at end of term, or temporarily extend service through a paid extended service term (EST).
As a reminder:
Effective May 4, 2026, subscriptions with the following criteria will automatically transition to EST on expiry:
Purchased or renewed on or after April 1, 2025
AND with an expiration date on or after May 4, 2026, and
Auto renew is set to off
If EST is not a desired end-of-term option, partners can select “cancel” or “renew” before the subscription’s end date.
Selecting “cancel” as an end-of-term option means the subscription ends on its expiration date, and the customer will lose access to the service.
Partners should prioritize renewal discussions with customers in advance of a subscription’s end date and discuss the best option for their business needs before the end of their service to avoid unexpected billing.
Learn more about EST, available resources, and how to export a list of EST subscriptions.
Update on multifactor authentication enforcement for Partner Center APIs
As previously communicated, multifactor authentication (MFA) enforcement for all Partner Center app+user APIs has started and will unfold in phases. API calls that do not support MFA may start to fail and return the following error:
HTTP Response Code: 401
Error Code: 900421
To avoid disruption, ensure your application integrations are updated to use MFA authentication for app+user scenarios.
Power Apps per app SKU returned to CSP price list in April 
As previously communicated, the Power Apps per app SKU was removed from the public price list effective January 2, 2026. This change was not intended to apply to the CSP channel; however, a temporary operational issue resulted in the SKU not appearing on CSP price lists in March 2026.
Microsoft has remedied this issue, and the Power Apps per app SKU is available again in the CSP price list. CSP partners and customers can continue to:
Purchase, use, and renew Power Apps per app subscriptions.
Add licenses for new customers and manage renewals, true-up, and true-down through standard CSP processes.
For additional details, please refer to the partner FAQ.
Now live: A clearer, automated path to the Support Services designation
As of March 31, 2026, partners can self-nominate for the Support Services designation in the Microsoft AI Cloud Partner Program and manage the entire enrollment experience from a single dashboard in Partner Center—giving you full visibility from audit to attainment and beyond.
The Support Services designation recognizes your organization for delivering world-class support to customers and empowering them to achieve more with Microsoft cloud solutions. Attaining this designation differentiates your organization in the market, reduces support costs, and unlocks high-impact business benefits for your business. Check out the Partner Center announcement for complete details about the automated experience and what to expect.
Validate your support capabilities and prepare for the future of partner support 
Unified for Partner (UfP), announced at Microsoft Ignite 2025, is Microsoft’s next-generation partner support offering. It modernizes partner support with revenue‑based pricing aligned to CSP cloud business and unlimited as-needed 24x7 reactive support for CSP workloads, and it’s driven by a partner‑first model where partners lead support and Microsoft acts as a backstop—supported by unified tooling, proactive services such as practice builders, and performance-based incentives.
UfP is being introduced through a deliberate, phased approach, beginning with pilots with select partners, followed by gradual expansion in H1 of fiscal year 2027 ahead of planned general availability in H2 of fiscal year 2027.
However, now is the time to lay the foundation for transforming your customer support. The Support Services designation is your starting point in this next chapter, giving customers a clear signal that you deliver proven, Microsoft-validated support.
Attain the designation to establish your credibility, gain a competitive edge, and unlock performance‑based discounts of up to 40% on UfP—along with expert guidance and support tools.
Next steps 
Learn more about how to attain the Support Services designation.
Review the partner data sheet to understand the scalable pricing model for UfP.
Stay tuned for future updates on UfP availability.
From pilots to production: How partners will drive Frontier Transformation 
AI has moved well beyond experimentation to production, and customers want AI that’s secure, governed, and scalable. Microsoft partners are turning that higher bar into opportunity—going beyond deployments to deliver durable outcomes with security at the center.
To support our ecosystem in driving this Frontier Transformation, Microsoft continues to invest in skilling, incentives, and go-to-market resources so you can build capability, scale repeatable offers, and deliver with confidence.
Read the State of the Partner Ecosystem to learn how AI is reshaping customer expectations, where partners are creating the most impact today, and how you can lead the next AI frontier.
Microsoft 365 pricing and packaging updates are coming in 2026—start renewal and upsell conversations now 
As previously announced, Microsoft is expanding the availability of security and management capabilities across commercial Microsoft 365 suites this year. Along with these added features, there will be a global pricing update across all purchasing channels effective July 1, 2026.
This is a key moment for you to drive renewals and upgrades ahead of the price update. Customers who renew before July 1 retain current pricing until their next renewal term, and you can unlock more revenue by attaching high‑value suites.
Lock in current pricing for Microsoft 365 Copilot Business bundles before July 1 or upsell to Microsoft 365 Business + Microsoft 365 Copilot Business bundles at renewal to support AI adoption for the small and medium enterprise customer segment.
Upsell to Microsoft 365 E3 or E5 for customers ready for advanced security, productivity, and IT management.
Activate incentives and targeted promotions from the Global Promo Readiness Guide to close deals before the pricing and packaging updates.
Successful renewal efforts can take up to three months, so now is the time to accelerate customer conversations and lock in price savings with one‑year or three‑year terms. Review the Partner Center announcement for more details and resources.
Explore ASPX: Turn customer signals into action in Partner Center 
ASPX (AI Business Solutions and Security Partner eXperience) delivers a unified view of customer usage, licensing, renewals, security readiness, and incentive eligibility—aligned with the same models used by Microsoft’s global sales force—so you can prioritize the right accounts and act with confidence.
As customers move from AI experimentation to execution, you can use ASPX to:
Identify Microsoft 365 Copilot adoption, renewal, and expansion opportunities earlier.
Surface security attach and upsell moments, including Microsoft 365 Business Premium with Defender and Purview, Microsoft 365 E3, and E5.
Align pre-sales and post-sales funded program offering eligibility to each customer to help improve win rates and deal velocity.
ASPX insights appear directly in Partner Center and through exports and APIs, so you can connect the data to your customer relationship management (CRM) system, business intelligence platform, and reporting tools.
Get started with ASPX 
Explore ASPX in Partner Center to identify priority customers and opportunities.
Operationalize insights using in-product views, exports, or APIs.
Integrate via API to deliver ASPX insights directly to sellers and reduce manual work.
Review the ASPX partner page for guidance and resources—including a walking deck and playbooks—to activate solution plays across Microsoft 365 Copilot, Agents at Work, and secure AI productivity.
For Security solution plays, review the ASPX walking deck and video.
Upcoming ASPX readiness events 
Making ASPX real: Partner showcase community call
Join us on April 28, 2026, to explore best practices and real-world examples of how partners use ASPX to improve execution and deliver measurable business impact.
8:00–9:00 AM Pacific Time (Americas/EMEA)
7:00–8:00 PM Pacific Time (Asia)
Four ways to build momentum and scale your security practice
Reduce early deal friction, create new attach opportunities, and build repeatable small and medium-sized business (SMB) motions.
Microsoft Sentinel 50-GB commitment tier promotion extended through June 30, 2026: Customers who purchase during this window lock in pricing through March 31, 2027—giving you a more predictable entry point for small and medium enterprise customers who want clarity on costs up front.
Save on Microsoft Purview Suite for Business Premium: Customers who have licensed Business Premium with Microsoft 365 Copilot Business or Microsoft 365 Copilot qualify for 50% off enhanced data protection on Purview Suite for Business Premium. This limited-time offer has been extended until July 1, 2026.
Expand customer reach with agents: Build and publish Microsoft Security Copilot agents in the Microsoft Security Store, making them discoverable within Security Copilot and Microsoft Security products.
Get SMB-ready talk tracks and tools in one place: Explore the Microsoft Security SMB partner hub for guidance, tools, and resources to lead stronger customer conversations and accelerate Microsoft Purview and Defender Suites for Business Premium scenarios.
For more details and updates, check out the Security partner newsletter.
Find and activate incentives in one place with the new digital incentives guide 
Now delivered through an online experience, the Microsoft Commercial Partner Incentives Guide gives you clear navigation, direct paths to engagement, and faster access to up-to-date guidance.
Search incentives by category to find opportunities that align with your go-to-market priorities.
Quickly confirm eligibility to make informed decisions about where to invest time and effort.
Act swiftly on opportunities with live links that connect you to the next action.
Explore the new digital incentives guide experience and share your feedback in our upcoming partner community calls (Americas/EMEA; Asia) as we continue to evolve the experience.
April partner investments updates: Eligibility changes and new resources
Explore the latest updates to plan your upcoming engagements with clearer eligibility and stronger alignment across solution areas.
Security engagements in Microsoft Commerce Incentives (MCI)
Education (EDU) customers are no longer eligible, keeping the program aligned to its intended commercial security scenarios and funding model.
Each customer organization is limited to two deliveries per engagement, reinforcing quality execution, balanced coverage, and broader partner participation.
AI Business Solutions engagements in MCI
Strategic 500 (S500) customers are no longer eligible, aligning to the Microsoft investment portfolio strategy.
Copilot + Power Accelerate remains eligible for S500 customers.
Co-op marketing guideline updates
We've updated the minimum co-op threshold associated with the Hosting incentive, as well as clarified sub-contracting language.
Azure Accelerate reminder
Partners with Azure specializations can earn Azure Accelerate investments when migrating eligible customers to Azure and when supporting software development companies as they migrate their customers. Review the terms and conditions in the incentives guide.
Localized Business Premium Immersion Briefing kits
Advanced Security for Business Premium Immersion Briefing kits are now available in French, Spanish (LATAM), Portuguese (Brazilian), German, Italian, and Japanese.
ISV Success Advanced: Build and Publish update 
Copilot Studio and Power Automate are now eligible workloads for the AI layer, expanding supported build scenarios. Review the terms and conditions in the incentives guide.
Build your AI fluency at a Sales Certification Week 
Our new three-day virtual Sales Certification Week is designed for partners to build AI fluency and turn AI conversations into real customer opportunities.
Participants will gain practical AI sales skills and prepare for the AB-730 (AI Fundamentals and Business Value) and AB-731 (AI Strategy, Adoption, and Governance) certifications. 
Register early—space is limited.
May 5–7 (EMEA - German) 
May 12–14 (Americas - Spanish) 
May 13–15 (Asia - Chinese,  Japanese, Korean) 
May 18–20 (EMEA - French) 
May 19–21 (Americas - Portuguese) 
More skilling resources to move your business forward 
Explore the new Partner Skilling Catalog to find on-demand training across Microsoft cloud solutions.
Move deals forward with Deal Ready Demo Accelerators—ready-to-demo scenarios built on Microsoft's latest technologies—and quickly demonstrate real customer outcomes without engineering effort or a live tenant.
Microsoft 365 Fundamentals retired: Stay on track with Modern Work skilling 
To keep the Microsoft AI Cloud Partner Program aligned with evolving market demand, we have retired the Microsoft 365 Fundamentals certification, effective March 31, 2026.
Microsoft 365 Fundamentals certifications earned before March 31, 2026, will continue to count toward skilling points for the Solutions Partner for Modern Work designation through March 31, 2027. We will continue to share guidance and new skilling options—including planned alternatives to Microsoft 365 Fundamentals like the Copilot and Agent Administration Fundamentals certification—to support your skilling journey.
Review certification requirements now so you can continue to qualify for designations and demonstrate your knowledge and skills to customers with confidence. We will continue providing resources and guidance to support your transition.

For the most updated list and priority, please visit this new Confluence page that I will be maintaining - https://aptum.atlassian.net/wiki/x/GQBEGwE
 
I would like to encourage you to please have a conversation with your respective teams and promote our need as a great personal development opportunity. For questions regarding training resources and potential reimbursement of exam costs, please contact Nikki. Any other questions please feel free to contact me.
 
Thank you.
 
Cheers,

Yes, please. Update the one you sent us (see below) with the migration services Aptum will be providing.

**
To support customers who require alternative colocation arrangements, Centrilogic has engaged Aptum as an approved service provider. Aptum operates data centre facilities in Heathrow and Portsmouth and is available to consult directly with affected customers to assess requirements and discuss transition options.
Available solutions include colocation services, private cloud offerings, and related infrastructure options. Aptum can also provide migration services, including planning and coordination support, to help customers transition their environments with minimal disruption. In some cases, migrating to Aptum may allow customers to retain their existing IP addresses, helping to reduce operational complexity.
Customers are under no obligation to use Aptum and remain free to pursue alternative arrangements with a provider of their choosing. This option is offered to provide flexibility and additional support during the transition process.
**

Thank you in advance.

Cc: Martin Mossakowski <mmossakowski@centrilogic.com>
Subject: Centrilogic & Aptum - Meeting Minutes – Bracknell update & Strategy - 26-03-30
 

Overview of Customer Footprint
The majority of customers are hosted in standard Centrilogic cabinets using shared power and carrier infrastructure.
One customer operates IBM mainframe equipment housed in non‑standard cabinets (wider and deeper), requiring confirmation of:
Power requirements
Weight and handling constraints
Only one customer (Perforce) operates within a dedicated cage (three cabinets plus expansion capacity).
Several customers also occupy office space in addition to data centre cabinets, which may influence their migration decisions.
 
Customer‑Specific Observations
Digital AI
Uses Centrilogic cabinets.
Requires physical separation between cabinets due to Wi‑Fi interference constraints.
Cloudian
Operates three cabinets with relatively high power consumption.
Also uses office space, though this is primarily for convenience.
It was agreed that, if migrating, the focus would likely be on rack space only, not office space.
Infinity
Uses one cabinet and office space.
Staff are onsite daily, making a migration to Portsmouth potentially less attractive.
OpenText
Uses six cabinets (five active, one for storage).
Makes frequent use of Smart Hands, which is included in their contract.
Perforce
Only cage customer at Bracknell.
Likely to seek an alternative location closer to their existing office, though requirements will be clarified in case migration is considered.
Telco providers (e.g., CityFibre, Colt)
Not expected to migrate to Aptum.
Will require time to remove or migrate their downstream customers from Bracknell.
 
Likelihood of Migration to Aptum
The general assessment is that more than 50% of customers may choose to migrate to Aptum.
A key differentiator will be the availability of migration assistance services, including:
De‑racking
Transportation
Re‑racking
Optional re‑cabling and Smart Hands support
Providing structured migration support is expected to significantly increase customer uptake.
 
Information Required to Progress

Aptum requested that the customer tracking spreadsheet include the following information for each customer: Number of cabinets and approximate REUs
Power provisioned and, where available, estimated power utilisation
Power feeds (A/B), number of circuits
Connectivity details:
Port speeds (1G, 10G)
Bandwidth utilisation
Use of Centrilogic carriers vs. customer‑owned circuits
IP address information:
IP blocks assigned (e.g. /29)
Confirmation of inclusion within the proposed /24 block
Contact status:
Whether the customer has already been contacted
By whom and when

 

Connectivity and IP Considerations
Most customers rely on Centrilogic carrier mix (Telia, LINX, Cogent, Virgin, Vodafone).
A small number of customers use customer‑owned last‑mile circuits (primarily BT or Virgin).
No major technical blockers were identified:
Aptum has sufficient space and power capacity.
Carrier compatibility appears feasible.
Follow‑up is required to:
Confirm power requirements for the IBM mainframe equipment.
Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility for migrating customers.
Confirm treatment of the /24 IP block for customer continuity.
 
Customer Communications
Two draft customer notification letters have been prepared but have not yet been sent.
Formal communication is intentionally being held until landlord agreements are finalised.
It was agreed that:
Aptum should not contact customers directly until a formal introduction is made by Centrilogic.
The draft letter will be shared with Aptum for review (already shared).
Aptum will contribute language describing:
Facility capabilities
Migration assistance services
The previously referenced June 30 date is not yet firm and remains subject to landlord agreement.
 
Key Agreements
No critical technical blockers were identified for the majority of customers.
Migration assistance is essential to the success of customer transitions.
The enriched customer spreadsheet is the critical dependency for next steps.
Early and coordinated communication will be key once formal notices are issued.
 
 
Action Items
Sergio B
Update and enrich the customer spreadsheet. - ONGOING
Share the spreadsheet and draft customer letter in the agreed shared folder. DONE
Aptum
Review the draft customer communication.
Provide proposed language covering facility details and migration services. @Bradley.Petrie@aptum.com,  @george.revie@aptum.com  would it be possible to receive the revised version by end of day today (3/30)?
Network & Facilities
Confirm power requirements for IBM mainframe equipment.
Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility.
All parties
Re‑engage once landlord agreements allow formal customer communication.
 

Please feel free to add any comments or suggestions.

From: Sergio Bernardou <sbernardou@centrilogic.com>
Sent: Monday, March 30, 2026 11:51 AM
To: Marc-Alexandre.Forget@aptum.com <Marc-Alexandre.Forget@aptum.com>; Bradley.Petrie@aptum.com <Bradley.Petrie@aptum.com>; Adam.Nelson@aptum.com <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; george.revie@aptum.com <george.revie@aptum.com>; Peter Bell <pbell@centrilogic.com>; Joshua Justice <jjustice@centrilogic.com>; Kevin Aplin <KAplin@centrilogic.com>; Karin Vierhuis <kvierhuis@centrilogic.com>; Mark Moore <MMoore@centrilogic.com>
Cc: Martin Mossakowski <mmossakowski@centrilogic.com>
Subject: Centrilogic & Aptum - Meeting Minutes – Bracknell update & Strategy - 26-03-30
 

Overview of Customer Footprint
The majority of customers are hosted in standard Centrilogic cabinets using shared power and carrier infrastructure.
One customer operates IBM mainframe equipment housed in non‑standard cabinets (wider and deeper), requiring confirmation of:
Power requirements
Weight and handling constraints
Only one customer (Perforce) operates within a dedicated cage (three cabinets plus expansion capacity).
Several customers also occupy office space in addition to data centre cabinets, which may influence their migration decisions.
 
Customer‑Specific Observations
Digital AI
Uses Centrilogic cabinets.
Requires physical separation between cabinets due to Wi‑Fi interference constraints.
Cloudian
Operates three cabinets with relatively high power consumption.
Also uses office space, though this is primarily for convenience.
It was agreed that, if migrating, the focus would likely be on rack space only, not office space.
Infinity
Uses one cabinet and office space.
Staff are onsite daily, making a migration to Portsmouth potentially less attractive.
OpenText
Uses six cabinets (five active, one for storage).
Makes frequent use of Smart Hands, which is included in their contract.
Perforce
Only cage customer at Bracknell.
Likely to seek an alternative location closer to their existing office, though requirements will be clarified in case migration is considered.
Telco providers (e.g., CityFibre, Colt)
Not expected to migrate to Aptum.
Will require time to remove or migrate their downstream customers from Bracknell.
 
Likelihood of Migration to Aptum
The general assessment is that more than 50% of customers may choose to migrate to Aptum.
A key differentiator will be the availability of migration assistance services, including:
De‑racking
Transportation
Re‑racking
Optional re‑cabling and Smart Hands support
Providing structured migration support is expected to significantly increase customer uptake.
 
Information Required to Progress

Aptum requested that the customer tracking spreadsheet include the following information for each customer: Number of cabinets and approximate REUs
Power provisioned and, where available, estimated power utilisation
Power feeds (A/B), number of circuits
Connectivity details:
Port speeds (1G, 10G)
Bandwidth utilisation
Use of Centrilogic carriers vs. customer‑owned circuits
IP address information:
IP blocks assigned (e.g. /29)
Confirmation of inclusion within the proposed /24 block
Contact status:
Whether the customer has already been contacted
By whom and when

 

Connectivity and IP Considerations
Most customers rely on Centrilogic carrier mix (Telia, LINX, Cogent, Virgin, Vodafone).
A small number of customers use customer‑owned last‑mile circuits (primarily BT or Virgin).
No major technical blockers were identified:
Aptum has sufficient space and power capacity.
Carrier compatibility appears feasible.
Follow‑up is required to:
Confirm power requirements for the IBM mainframe equipment.
Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility for migrating customers.
Confirm treatment of the /24 IP block for customer continuity.
 
Customer Communications
Two draft customer notification letters have been prepared but have not yet been sent.
Formal communication is intentionally being held until landlord agreements are finalised.
It was agreed that:
Aptum should not contact customers directly until a formal introduction is made by Centrilogic.
The draft letter will be shared with Aptum for review (already shared).
Aptum will contribute language describing:
Facility capabilities
Migration assistance services
The previously referenced June 30 date is not yet firm and remains subject to landlord agreement.
 
Key Agreements
No critical technical blockers were identified for the majority of customers.
Migration assistance is essential to the success of customer transitions.
The enriched customer spreadsheet is the critical dependency for next steps.
Early and coordinated communication will be key once formal notices are issued.
 
 
Action Items
Sergio B
Update and enrich the customer spreadsheet. - ONGOING
Share the spreadsheet and draft customer letter in the agreed shared folder. DONE
Aptum
Review the draft customer communication.
Provide proposed language covering facility details and migration services. @Bradley.Petrie@aptum.com,  @george.revie@aptum.com  would it be possible to receive the revised version by end of day today (3/30)?
Network & Facilities
Confirm power requirements for IBM mainframe equipment.
Validate carrier mix and cross‑connect compatibility.
All parties
Re‑engage once landlord agreements allow formal customer communication.
 

Please feel free to add any comments or suggestions.
SERGIO BERNARDOU
Project Management Consultant
 
sbernardou@centrilogic.com
www.centrilogic.com
Follow us: Linkedin | X

From: Ian Rae <Ian.Rae@aptum.com>
Sent: Tuesday, April 21, 2026 14:05
To: Andy Alvarado <andy.alvarado@basis.com>
Cc: Michael Freiman <michael.freiman@basis.com>; David Blankinship <David.Blankinship@aptum.com>
Subject: today's incident 
 

Hi Andy,

The matter of an unauthorized change management with your AWS account today has been raised to me.

I understand there was some motivation to get the transition to the new distributor (TD Synnex) in place to address the concerns with Ingram's past behaviour, ensuring there is no root access from distribution, and also to make sure we can pass on the increased margin once we hit May invoicing.

In doing so it appears the transition to the new distributor was initiated without your awareness. While this should have no tangible impact on your environments operationally, this is a completely unacceptable situation and should have been performed via scheduled change management with your approval and involvement.

Adam Nelson our VP Operations promptly escalated this issue to me and I have asked him to review and advise me personally as to root cause and remediation.

I want to apologize sincerely for the situation, and will circle back once we understand what happened and why, and what we propose doing about it.

 cc David and Michael

Ian

Ian Rae
President & CEO
+1 (514) 944-4008
aptum.com | LinkedIn

items.subject: (CMI) FW: FCMI #2 - FW: Changing Collation of SQL Server Instance
items.body_text:
[Barbara Krzywoszanski]
Original Request:

We’re currently in the final stages of testing and we have discovered an issue caused by the SQL Server’s collation being set to SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS, where it actually needs to be Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS.  

Recap from Donald

This one is effectively a rebuild. With SQL Server, there are two practical paths here: patch around the collation mismatch, which tends to keep causing issues with temp tables and other server-level behavior, or correct the instance collation properly by rebuilding it with the required setting.

Work is outlined in yellow below.

Estimated # of Hour - 60 to 75 hours

Pat agrees with Donald’s assessment. 

Marcus provided information for CMI to do the change themselves. 

Outcome of the rebuild

Demonstrating to CMI that we got it wrong from the beginning. 

There will likely be credits for their Azure usage since they can’t go live. 

The number of hours that we’ll be investing into it = appx $20K of services @ $275 blended rate. 

Customer will have a stable environment

Barbara Krzywoszanski
Service Delivery Manager
+1 (514) 673-7542
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Donald Donovan <Donald.Donovan@aptum.com>
Date: Monday, April 20, 2026 at 11:57 AM
To: Barbara Krzywoszanski <Barbara.Krzywoszanski@aptum.com>; Patrick Wolthausen <Patrick.Wolthausen@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: FCMI #2 - FW: Changing Collation of SQL Server Instance

This is the dangerous option I wanted to avoid and is exactly why they're having problems now to be frank.

That July remediation may have corrected the user database and AG synchronization, but not the SQL Server instance collation itself. If SQL Server was not rebuilt with the required server collation at that time, which that thread implies, then the current issue is a continuation of that incomplete remediation rather than a new, separate collation problem.

Donald Donovan
Serivice Delivery Contractor
+1 (438) 802-5139
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Barbara Krzywoszanski <Barbara.Krzywoszanski@aptum.com>
Sent: Monday, April 20, 2026 11:02 AM
To: Donald Donovan <Donald.Donovan@aptum.com>; Patrick Wolthausen <Patrick.Wolthausen@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: FCMI #2 - FW: Changing Collation of SQL Server Instance
 
Donald, 

I reached out to Marcus about their request trying to trace the original instructions. 

Marcus has already provided them information on this topic (see attached email) and they seem to have done the switch. 

Are you able to tell me if this is something different or they’re trying to revert back to their original config? 

Barbara Krzywoszanski
Service Delivery Manager
+1 (514) 673-7542
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Donald Donovan <Donald.Donovan@aptum.com>
Date: Sunday, April 19, 2026 at 7:32 PM
To: Barbara Krzywoszanski <Barbara.Krzywoszanski@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: FCMI #2 - FW: Changing Collation of SQL Server Instance

TL;DR: This is a rebuild, not a configuration switch.
This one is effectively a rebuild. With SQL Server, there are two practical paths here: patch around the collation mismatch, which tends to keep causing issues with temp tables and other server-level behavior, or correct the instance collation properly by rebuilding it with the required setting.
I’m assuming we want to do the second option now, while the systems are not user-facing. If we only patch around it now, we are likely setting ourselves up for another remediation later, potentially after cutover or once the environment is in production, when the same work becomes much harder and higher risk.

Change Order Scope: SQL Server Collation Correction
Correct the SQL Server instance collation on the three non-production database servers so the server-level collation matches the required application/database collation.
Current instance collation is SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS. Target instance collation is Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS.
Affected servers:

cmet-rcy-db-1

cmet-rcy-db-2

cmet-rcy-db-dr
Because these systems are not live, the recommended approach is an outage and rebuild of the SQL Server system databases rather than a live migration. Existing user databases, schemas, and data will be preserved. Server-level objects will be exported before the rebuild and recreated afterward.
Work Included
Before making changes, the existing SQL Server configuration will be reviewed and captured. This includes the current database layout, SQL Server settings, logins, permissions, SQL Agent jobs, schedules, credentials, linked servers, Availability Group configuration, and replication configuration.
Backups or snapshots will be taken before the rebuild so the environment can be recovered if required. User databases will be backed up or otherwise preserved before SQL Server system databases are rebuilt.
Availability Group and replication components will be temporarily removed, disabled, or torn down as required to allow the SQL Server instances to be rebuilt cleanly. SQL Agent jobs and related automation will also be disabled during the change.
The SQL Server system databases will be rebuilt on each affected server using the required target collation. This corrects the server-level collation, including system database and tempdb behavior.
After the rebuild, existing user databases will be restored or reattached. SQL Server logins, permissions, SQL Agent jobs, schedules, credentials, linked servers, and other required instance-level objects will be recreated.
The Availability Group configuration will be rebuilt and validated across the affected servers.
Replication will be recreated and validated, including the required publisher, distributor, subscriber, publications, subscriptions, and replication agent jobs.
Post-change validation will confirm that the SQL Server instance collation is correct, databases are online, logins and jobs are functioning, Availability Group synchronization is healthy, replication is operating correctly, and basic application/database smoke testing is successful.
Estimated Effort

Work Area
Estimated Effort

Discovery, configuration capture, and backup preparation
6–8 hours

Temporary teardown of AG, replication, and SQL Agent dependencies
4–6 hours

SQL Server system database rebuild across three servers
6–9 hours

Restore or reattach user databases
5–8 hours

Recreate logins, jobs, credentials, linked servers, and server configuration
8–12 hours

Rebuild and validate Availability Group configuration
5–8 hours

Recreate and validate replication
10–15 hours

Post-change testing, cleanup, and issue resolution
6–8 hours

Total Estimated Effort 50–72 engineering hours
This includes discovery, backups, SQL Server system database rebuilds across three servers, restoration of user databases, recreation of server-level objects, Availability Group rebuild, replication rebuild if required, and post-change validation.
Because the systems are not currently user-facing, no live cutover engineering is required; however, the environment contains significant SQL Server configuration, jobs, HA components, and possible replication dependencies. A 25% contingency is recommended for validation issues, missing dependencies, or replication rework. In theory we do not need these, but they are good to have in case.

Donald Donovan
Serivice Delivery Contractor
+1 (438) 802-5139
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Barbara Krzywoszanski <Barbara.Krzywoszanski@aptum.com>
Sent: Tuesday, April 14, 2026 2:59 PM
To: Donald Donovan <Donald.Donovan@aptum.com>
Subject: FCMI #2 - FW: Changing Collation of SQL Server Instance
 

Barbara Krzywoszanski
Service Delivery Manager
+1 (514) 673-7542
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Chris Sorensen <csorensen@combinedmetal.com>
Date: Tuesday, April 14, 2026 at 12:48 PM
To: Aptum Customer Care <customercare@aptum.atlassian.net>
Cc: Barbara Krzywoszanski <barbara.krzywoszanski@aptum.com>, Keith Schlotzhauer <kschlotzhauer@combinedmetal.com>
Subject: Changing Collation of SQL Server Instance

Hi,
 
We had MSSQL Server installed on several of our servers as part of a project Aptum did for us.  We’re currently in the final stages of testing and we have discovered an issue caused by the SQL Server’s collation being set to SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS, where it actually needs to be Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS.  The databases themselves have the proper collation, so everything up until now had seemed to work, but testing today encountered an error caused by this, and we need it changed.
 
The servers are part of an availability group tied to an Azure load balancer, and have server-level logins, jobs and publishing set up.  I believe changing the collation will wipe all of that out.  Is there any way to change the collation and then restore all the server-level items to it without impacting everything else we’ve done?
 
Servers:
cmet-recy-db-1
cmet-recy-db-2
cmet-recy-db-dr
 

 
 

Chris Sorensen
Director, IT & Infrastructure
Combined Metal Industries

 csorensen@combinedmetal.com 

 416-684-2585
 8470 Keele St, Vaughan, ON L4K 2S1

We take this scrap seriously.

TM
 
Please consider the environment before printing this e-mail. This communication may contain confidential and/or privileged information intended only for the person(s) to whom it is addressed. Any unauthorized disclosure, copying, other distribution of this communication or taking any action on its contents is strictly prohibited. If you have received this message in error, please notify Combined Metal Industries Inc. immediately and delete this message without reading, copying or forwarding it to anyone.
items.body_snippet:
[Barbara Krzywoszanski]
Original Request:

We’re currently in the final stages of testing and we have discovered an issue caused by the SQL Server’s collation being set to SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS, where it actually needs to be Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS.  

Recap from Donald

This one is effectively a rebuild. With SQL Server, there are two practical paths here: patch around the collation mismatch, which tends to keep causing issues with temp tables and other server-level behavior, or correct

items.subject: Re: Azure Arc for Internal IT SQL hourly usage
items.body_text:
[Martin Tessier]
Hi Andy and Adam,

For Internal IT, Fred can surely add more if the situation as changes, but we are not paying for MS SQL. We were paying for SA previously so we can upgrade our servers and for Mobility capability.  Since we are not using Cherwell anymore, there was no good reason for us to continue paying ( note that BI infrastructure is also using MS SQL but doesn't require any of the paid feature ).
  

Martin Tessier
Director, Hosting
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>
Sent: Tuesday, April 21, 2026 10:34 AM
To: Andy Petterson <Andy.Petterson@aptum.com>; Martin Tessier <Martin.Tessier@aptum.com>
Cc: Fred Taylor-Young <Fred.Taylor-Young@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: Azure Arc for Internal IT SQL hourly usage 

Asking because I got this email from MS:

Hi Adam,
 
Because you license SQL Server monthly with your SPLA Agreement, we wanted to make sure you were aware there's a lower-cost option that requires no data migration, no infrastructure changes, and zero downtime.
 
SQL Server enabled by Azure Arc is a simpler, pay-as-you-go licensing model at a lower cost than what you are paying for SQL Server in SPLA today. And you can take advantage of it immediately, keeping your workloads exactly where they are.
 
Just reply to this email and we'll set up a quick call to walk through what this looks like for your environment.
 
Regards,
 
Maggie Hedges
v-mahedges@microsoft.com
SQL Arc Licensing

Adam Nelson
Vice President, Operations
+1 (438) 802-9796
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Andy Petterson <Andy.Petterson@aptum.com>
Sent: April 21, 2026 10:31
To: Martin Tessier <Martin.Tessier@aptum.com>
Cc: Fred Taylor-Young <Fred.Taylor-Young@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>
Subject: Azure Arc for Internal IT SQL hourly usage
 
Hi Martin,

I know we took a look at this a few years ago, but Adam was asking if there is any point in using Azure Arc for our internal SQL instead of our standard SQL licensing?

From memory, are we only using it for Cherwell? I'm assuming we've ramped down the SQL cores and maybe just have one SQL server instead of two in an HA, and are paying yearly for those?

Depending on when the renewal window is, we could transfer this to Azure Arc, and then that would be paying hourly for however many cores are in the SQL server (min 4 cores). Not sure if you were planning on stopping the services and just starting them when someone needed to go in for a bit or getting rid of the servers altogether, this would mean that while the SQL services were stopped we would pay $0 in SQL license charges until they started again. 

Let me know if IT are interested in this and we can go over it.

Thanks,

Andy Petterson
Solutions Architect
+442380926091
aptum.com | LinkedIn

---

Matthew and Team,

 

Wanted to let you know that I have a meeting Monday morning to discuss the billing process approval for Aptum.

 

Are there any updates from your side?

 

 

Stephen Slattery

From: Stephen Slattery <sslattery@pax8.com>

Cc: Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: [External] Re: Pax8 Follow Up 

 

Matthew,

 
We'd like to give an update on Billing Transfer and Marketing partnership.
 
Billing Transfer:
 
The April 1st commitment would not be a general availability experience so there are some things to consider.
    We cannot test a customer because AWS does not allow for a APN partner to straddle two distributors.
    Aptum would have to agree to an AWS Distributor Seller Agreement with Pax8 (see Onboarding Guide).  
    Billing will not be granular in the beginning.  There'll be summaries of costs but not individual line-item billing attributes.  This is an AWS limitation with their new billing transfer platform.
    8% off eligible billing attributes will have to be manual on the Pax8 in the beginning due to an AWS issue we caught as part of the pilot program.  I'm working on getting internal approval for that next week.  
    8% would apply to April's usage billing in May.
    From a technical perspective, Pax8 is set up for your move to the new AWS billing transfer platform. 
 
Here's the customer move process overview:
·                     Aptum creates a Program Management Account (PMA) in their AWS Console
·                     Pax8 send a billing transfer request to PMA and Aptum accepts
·                     Aptum creates a PMA in each end customer AWS Console
·                     Aptum sends a billing transfer request to end customer(s) and they accept
·                     Create the relationship in the APN
·                     Pax8 has no need to contact the end customer(s).
 
We can set up a call to dive deeper into this when we are ready and to show  your team the Pax8 marketplace with the invoice UI.
 
I'm sure your Team will have questions, and the expectation is we might not have all the answers as we continue to integrate and build the billing transfer platform moving towards general availability. 

 

Marketing Update:

 
For our MDF, it pertains to MSPs who host their own customer or prospect events, or who exhibit at/attend industry events with the purpose of generating business and ROI.
 
We do have an approval process here that requires you to provide details prior to our approval granted, and reimbursement is delivered after the event has been completed.
 
As for the Premier Program, those are marketing assets (hospitality suite access, tickets, branding, etc) with local sports franchises, negotiated by our team on your behalf. The program does not operate on a set % of your spend. It will depend on the spend, the franchise you wish to work with, and more. To nail that down to something more tangible, I'd want you to have a discussion with our Program Director, Leigh Nordhoff. I am happy to set that up anytime.

 

Let me know if there's a path forward here and then I'll follow up on my final approvals.  I really want to partner with Aptum and if we can get through this together, it'll be a testament to our partnership.  Can we earn your business?  

 

Thank you. 

 

Stephen Slattery

Director of Solutions Consulting

From: Stephen Slattery <sslattery@pax8.com>
Sent: Friday, February 6, 2026 10:08 AM

Cc: Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: [External] Re: Pax8 Follow Up 

 

Matthew,

 
We'd like to give an update on Billing Transfer and Marketing partnership.
 
Billing Transfer:
 
The April 1st commitment would not be a general availability experience so there are some things to consider.
    We cannot test a customer because AWS does not allow for a APN partner to straddle two distributors.
    Aptum would have to agree to an AWS Distributor Seller Agreement with Pax8 (see Onboarding Guide).  
    Billing will not be granular in the beginning.  There'll be summaries of costs but not individual line-item billing attributes.  This is an AWS limitation with their new billing transfer platform.
    8% off eligible billing attributes will have to be manual on the Pax8 in the beginning due to an AWS issue we caught as part of the pilot program.  I'm working on getting internal approval for that next week.  
    8% would apply to April's usage billing in May.
    From a technical perspective, Pax8 is set up for your move to the new AWS billing transfer platform. 
 
Here's the customer move process overview:
·                     Aptum creates a Program Management Account (PMA) in their AWS Console
·                     Pax8 send a billing transfer request to PMA and Aptum accepts
·                     Aptum creates a PMA in each end customer AWS Console
·                     Aptum sends a billing transfer request to end customer(s) and they accept
·                     Create the relationship in the APN
·                     Pax8 has no need to contact the end customer(s).
 
We can set up a call to dive deeper into this when we are ready and to show  your team the Pax8 marketplace with the invoice UI.
 
I'm sure your Team will have questions, and the expectation is we might not have all the answers as we continue to integrate and build the billing transfer platform moving towards general availability. 

 

Marketing Update:

 
For our MDF, it pertains to MSPs who host their own customer or prospect events, or who exhibit at/attend industry events with the purpose of generating business and ROI.
 
We do have an approval process here that requires you to provide details prior to our approval granted, and reimbursement is delivered after the event has been completed.
 
As for the Premier Program, those are marketing assets (hospitality suite access, tickets, branding, etc) with local sports franchises, negotiated by our team on your behalf. The program does not operate on a set % of your spend. It will depend on the spend, the franchise you wish to work with, and more. To nail that down to something more tangible, I'd want you to have a discussion with our Program Director, Leigh Nordhoff. I am happy to set that up anytime.

 

Let me know if there's a path forward here and then I'll follow up on my final approvals.  I really want to partner with Aptum and if we can get through this together, it'll be a testament to our partnership.  Can we earn your business?  

 

Thank you. 

 

Stephen Slattery

To: David Blankinship <David.Blankinship@aptum.com>
Cc: Barbara Krzywoszanski <Barbara.Krzywoszanski@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Ian Rae <Ian.Rae@aptum.com>; Sarah Blanchard <Sarah.Blanchard@aptum.com>; Erik Tanguay <Erik.Tanguay@aptum.com>
Subject: FW: Update on AWS pricing
 
David,
 
You can let Andy know that, effective April 1, 2026 (April AWS usage), Aptum will apply a 3% discount to AWS applicable items. 
 
Somebody’s for Adam’s team will share the specifics (eligibility subset of Consumption that qualifies) before end of April.
 
Nothing for you to ‘renegotiate’.
 
MA
 

Marc Alex Forget

Senior Vice-President of Sales and Marketing

aptum.com | LinkedIn

 

m: Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com> 
Sent: Wednesday, January 14, 2026 9:05 AM
To: Andy Alvarado <Andy.Alvarado@basis.com>
Cc: David Blankinship <David.Blankinship@aptum.com>; Marc Alex Forget <Marc-Alexandre.Forget@aptum.com>; Patrick Wolthausen <Patrick.Wolthausen@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: Update on AWS pricing
 

Hi Andy, 

Good question. There is a subset of Consumption that qualifies, and this is an area that may have change coming. 

Current Qualifying Consumption:

Core Compute & Storage: EC2, S3, RDS, Lambda, VPC, etc.

Data Transfer: Standard data transfer fees associated with Authorized Services.

Usage Types: Both On-Demand usage and usage covered by Savings Plans (SP) or Reserved Instances (RI) usually qualify, though the discount is applied to the effective price paid.

Excluded / Non-Qualifying Consumption:

AWS Marketplace: Software, data, or services bought from third-party vendors via the Marketplace are excluded.

Specific "Pass-Through" Services: Services where AWS acts as a carrier or reseller often have 0% discount. Common examples include:

Amazon Connect (Telephony/Carrier fees)

Route 53 (Domain Registration fees)

Specific GenAI Models: Some third-party models on Amazon Bedrock (e.g., Anthropic, Cohere) may be ineligible for standard program discounts.

Support Charges: AWS Support (Business/Enterprise) fees are often calculated separately and may have their own specific discount structure (or none), distinct from the usage discount.

Taxes & Upfront Fees: Taxes (VAT/GST) and one-time upfront fees (like the upfront portion of an All-Upfront RI) are not "consumption" and do not get the usage discount percentage.

 

Regards,

 

Marc Pare

Vice President, Cloud Platforms
+1 (514) 226-1954
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Andy Alvarado <Andy.Alvarado@basis.com>
Sent: Tuesday, January 13, 2026 1:11 PM
To: Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>
Cc: David Blankinship <David.Blankinship@aptum.com>; Marc Alex Forget <Marc-Alexandre.Forget@aptum.com>; Patrick Wolthausen <Patrick.Wolthausen@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: Update on AWS pricing 

 

Another question...

 

Is this 3% on the total bill?

 

For example, I have several vendors I contract through AWS Marketplace.  Are those agreements subject to this discount?  Or is this confined to AWS native services (EC2, RDS, S3, et al).

 

Thanks.

 

Andy

From: Andy Alvarado <Andy.Alvarado@basis.com>
Sent: Tuesday, January 13, 2026 12:09
To: Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>
Cc: David Blankinship <David.Blankinship@aptum.com>; Marc Alex Forget <Marc-Alexandre.Forget@aptum.com>; Patrick Wolthausen <Patrick.Wolthausen@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: Update on AWS pricing 

 

Hello Marc.  Thanks for the info.

 

I am OoO until Thursday.  You can start preparing the paperwork.

 

If you can provide more information about your following comments, I would appreciate it.  I want to ensure I set expectations correctly (internally).

 

- if they (AWS and Pax8) change the program and we get less than 4%, we will need to revisit how to proceed

I am assuming this would result in renegotiation and simply a reduction in our discount.  If there are other considerations, please elaborate.

 

- There have been multiple changes in the AWS Distribution Program and may have more coming that we are trying to confirm. 

Do you foresee changes that would cause something beyond a reduction in discount?  Is complete termination of the agreement b/c it is no longer logical (profitable) something I should consider a possibility?

 

Is Friday morning best for a chat?  If it works for everyone else, send an invite. 

 

Thanks.

 

-AA

From: Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>
Sent: Tuesday, January 13, 2026 08:12
To: Andy Alvarado <Andy.Alvarado@basis.com>
Cc: David Blankinship <David.Blankinship@aptum.com>; Marc Alex Forget <Marc-Alexandre.Forget@aptum.com>; Patrick Wolthausen <Patrick.Wolthausen@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: Update on AWS pricing 

 

Hi Andy, 
I hope the holidays were good to you. I confirmed yesterday we can still offer you the 3% but will need some language to make it clear if they (AWS and Pax8) change the program and we get less than 4%, we will need to revisit how to proceed. 
This year ended and started with some additional challenges on the AWS Distribution Program side that I thought we had landed last year. We thought we had an agreement with Pax8 to get an equitable split but the AWS and Pax8 programs are not within our control and leave us with 1% in the pursuit of providing you the 3% we proposed. 
I spoke with Ian Rae and our broader team, and we have agreed to proceed with that split.
I am happy to support a call at your earliest convenience to discuss further.
To give you an idea of what that 3% savings actually could look like for 2026. I think we should review the type of AWS spend that will qualify and plan for an April 1st start. There have been multiple changes in the AWS Distribution Program and may have more coming that we are trying to confirm. 
What is your availability for a call to discuss or if you prefer, we can just work on preparing the paperwork?
Best regards,
Marc

Taxes & Upfront Fees: Taxes (VAT/GST) and one-time upfront fees (like the upfront portion of an All-Upfront RI) are not "consumption" and do not get the usage discount percentage.

 

Regards,

 

Marc Pare

Vice President, Cloud Platforms
+1 (514) 226-1954
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Andy Alvarado <Andy.Alvarado@basis.com>
Sent: Tuesday, January 13, 2026 1:11 PM
To: Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>
Cc: David Blankinship <David.Blankinship@aptum.com>; Marc Alex Forget <Marc-Alexandre.Forget@aptum.com>; Patrick Wolthausen <Patrick.Wolthausen@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: Update on AWS pricing 

 

Another question...

 

Is this 3% on the total bill?

 

For example, I have several vendors I contract through AWS Marketplace.  Are those agreements subject to this discount?  Or is this confined to AWS native services (EC2, RDS, S3, et al).

 

Thanks.

 

Andy

From: Andy Alvarado <Andy.Alvarado@basis.com>
Sent: Tuesday, January 13, 2026 12:09
To: Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>
Cc: David Blankinship <David.Blankinship@aptum.com>; Marc Alex Forget <Marc-Alexandre.Forget@aptum.com>; Patrick Wolthausen <Patrick.Wolthausen@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: Update on AWS pricing 

 

Hello Marc.  Thanks for the info.

 

I am OoO until Thursday.  You can start preparing the paperwork.

 

If you can provide more information about your following comments, I would appreciate it.  I want to ensure I set expectations correctly (internally).

 

- if they (AWS and Pax8) change the program and we get less than 4%, we will need to revisit how to proceed

I am assuming this would result in renegotiation and simply a reduction in our discount.  If there are other considerations, please elaborate.

 

- There have been multiple changes in the AWS Distribution Program and may have more coming that we are trying to confirm. 

Do you foresee changes that would cause something beyond a reduction in discount?  Is complete termination of the agreement b/c it is no longer logical (profitable) something I should consider a possibility?

 

Is Friday morning best for a chat?  If it works for everyone else, send an invite. 

 

Thanks.

 

-AA

From: Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>
Sent: Tuesday, January 13, 2026 08:12
To: Andy Alvarado <Andy.Alvarado@basis.com>
Cc: David Blankinship <David.Blankinship@aptum.com>; Marc Alex Forget <Marc-Alexandre.Forget@aptum.com>; Patrick Wolthausen <Patrick.Wolthausen@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: Update on AWS pricing 

 

Hi Andy, 
I hope the holidays were good to you. I confirmed yesterday we can still offer you the 3% but will need some language to make it clear if they (AWS and Pax8) change the program and we get less than 4%, we will need to revisit how to proceed. 
This year ended and started with some additional challenges on the AWS Distribution Program side that I thought we had landed last year. We thought we had an agreement with Pax8 to get an equitable split but the AWS and Pax8 programs are not within our control and leave us with 1% in the pursuit of providing you the 3% we proposed. 
I spoke with Ian Rae and our broader team, and we have agreed to proceed with that split.
I am happy to support a call at your earliest convenience to discuss further.
To give you an idea of what that 3% savings actually could look like for 2026. I think we should review the type of AWS spend that will qualify and plan for an April 1st start. There have been multiple changes in the AWS Distribution Program and may have more coming that we are trying to confirm. 
What is your availability for a call to discuss or if you prefer, we can just work on preparing the paperwork?
Best regards,
Marc

 

This email is sent for and on behalf of Aptum Managed Services (Canada) Inc with its head office at 305 Milner Avenue, Suite 912, Toronto, Ontario, M1B 3V4, Canada. This email and any attachments are confidential and intended for the recipient only. On Aptum's expense invoice our vendor (currently Ingram Micro,  will be TDS soon)  will provide an additional 3% discount to Aptum for total consumption.

Subject: RE: Basis Additional Discount for AWS Consumption 

 

Hi Adam,
My understanding is that Marc-Alex and David are meeting Basis on Wednesday. I’m trying to finalize details prior, but the additional discount will not be on all AWS services. 
 

Matthew Carter

VP, Project Management & Global Supply Chain

From: Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com> 
Sent: April 20, 2026 3:05 PM
To: Dennis Lam <Dennis.Lam@aptum.com>; Priscillia Pare <Priscillia.Pare@aptum.com>; Barbara Krzywoszanski <Barbara.Krzywoszanski@aptum.com>; Lacie Allen-Morley <Lacie.Allen-Morley@aptum.com>; Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>
Cc: Erik Tanguay <Erik.Tanguay@aptum.com>; Sarah Blanchard <Sarah.Blanchard@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: Basis Additional Discount for AWS Consumption
 

We have committed to Basis the 3% as of April 1. 

 

I say we keep that expectation. 

 

We told them that we would reimburse them (maybe not EXACTLY on May 1) for April's consumption (the 3%). 

 

Adam Nelson

Vice President, Operations
+1 (438) 802-9796
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Dennis Lam <Dennis.Lam@aptum.com>
Sent: April 13, 2026 14:51
To: Priscillia Pare <Priscillia.Pare@aptum.com>; Barbara Krzywoszanski <Barbara.Krzywoszanski@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Lacie Allen-Morley <Lacie.Allen-Morley@aptum.com>; Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>
Cc: Erik Tanguay <Erik.Tanguay@aptum.com>; Sarah Blanchard <Sarah.Blanchard@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: Basis Additional Discount for AWS Consumption 

 

Hi Priscillia,

On Aptum's expense invoice our vendor (currently Ingram Micro,  will be TDS soon)  will provide an additional 3% discount to Aptum for total consumption.      

On Aptum's revenue side,  we will be giving Basis (Aptum's customer) a 3% discount.   This discount will only apply to Basis.

There shouldnt be any "doubling",  as we are talking about revenue vs. expenses.

 

 

@Adam,

Finance would suggest waiting for discounts on the expense side, before applying additional discount to basis + any catchup.    However,  AWS has officially updated their partner program as of Apr 1st  and Aptum should receive 3% regardless of which vendor we choose,  so its your decision if you want to apply the discount to Basis immediately,  or let Basis know the discounts will arrive next month and there will be a catchup.

 

 

 

thanks,

Dennis Lam

Manager, Finance
+1 (604) 909-6386
